decision_note: promote strong historical medicinal plant family from review into the seed shortlist

supporting_works.title: Botanic guide to health, and the natural pathology of disease / by A.I. Coffin.
Botanic guide to health, and the natural pathology of disease / by A.I. Coffin.
A botanic guide to health and the natural pathology of disease / [Albert Isaiah Coffin].
A botanic guide to health and the natural pathology of disease / [Albert Isaiah Coffin].
Treatise on midwifery and the diseases of women and children with remedies / by A. I. Coffin.

A supplement to the pharmacopoeia : being a treatise on pharmacology in general; including not only the drugs and compounds which are used by practitioners of medicine, but also those which are sold by chemists, druggists, and herbalists, for other purposes; together with a collection of the most useful medical formulæ ; an explanation of the contractions used by physicians and druggists; the medical arrangement of the articles of the London pharmacopeia, with their doses, at one view; a similar list of the indigenous plants of the British islands, which are capable of being used in medicine, &c. ; and also a very copious index, English and Latin, of the various names by which the articles have been known at different periods. / by Samuel Frederick Gray.
Medical reform, or, Physiology and botanic practice for the people / John Stevens.
The vegetable materia medica and practice of medicine / by ... Abel Tennant.  Containing in detail his practical knowledge of American remedies, in curing diseases.
A retail price list of herbs, roots, barks, and botanic medicines ... to be had at Dale's Botanic Medical Hall ... Glasgow.

An introduction to physic and surgery: containing. I. Medicinal institutions, or physiology, hygieine, pathology, semeiotice, and therapeutice. II. A compendious system of anatomy III. A general account of woods, ulcers, tumours, operations, and dressings; as also, the medicines used in surgery. IV. Botany and the materia medica. V. Pharmacy, as well chemical as Galenical. VI. A dispensatory, containing a great variety of the most efficacious officinal compositions, and extemporaneous prescriptions. VII. A syllabus of the symptoms of all the diseases incident to the human body. VIII. An index of diseases and their remedies. IX. A posologic table, containing the doses of all the simples and compounds. X. An explanation of the terms of art, accented as they are to be pronounced. / By R. Brookes, M.D.

overview.title: The guide to health and married woman's companion : a plain, practical and comprehensive treatise upon the practice of medicine : in the treatment of the most common forms of disease to which the human family is subject, with a concise direction for the preparation and use of a numerous collection of the vegetables made use of, and directions for preparing and adminstering them to cure diseases, and an appendix devoted to the treatment of diseases in horses
overview.excerpt: First give an emetic. After the stomach has become quiet, take foxglove, pulverized, a teaspoonful, add half a pint of boiling water; cover till cold. Dose, a table- spoonful every two hours; if it produces nausea or...

overview.title: Carleton's household physician : a family medical adviser, containing knowledge that will promote health, cure disease and prolong life, describing all diseases and teaching how to cure them by the simplest medicines : also, an analysis of everything related to courtship, marriage, and the production, management, and rearing of healthy families, together with a chapter on the preparation of medicines, giving prescriptions and valuable receipts, with a section explaining the botanical practice and the popular use of herbs

retrieval_text: Yarrow is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 52 works and 102 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Yarrow, Yarrow Herb. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Leaf, Flower, Bark, Seed, Berry. Common preparation co-mentions include Tea, Decoction, Bath, Juice, Powder, Emetic. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Wounds or ulcers, Fever, Rheumatism, Inflammation, Consumption. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Bleeding caution, Child-use caution, Fatal-risk warning, Abortifacient warning. Representative source works include Botanic guide to health, and the natural pathology of disease / by A.I. Coffin.; Botanic guide to health, and the natural pathology of disease / by A.I. Coffin.; A botanic guide to health and the natural pathology of disease / [Albert Isaiah Coffin].; A botanic guide to health and the natural pathology of disease / [Albert Isaiah Coffin].. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
